body.title: Memory architecture: 4-tier scope model (global/local/agent/client)
body.result: 4-tier memory scope design doc + db_proxy factories (make_global_proxy, make_agent_proxy, make_client_proxy) + 11 unit tests; bus access control model documented; migration path from Igor-wild-0001 described
body.description:
Design and implement the 4-tier memory scope model for the rack:

- **Global DB** — universal patterns, how-to, bootstraps. Shared across all UU instances. Read by all agents. Write governance-controlled (not free-for-all). This is the compiled inference commons.
- **Local/Instance DB** — 'how did we do last week.' All non-blocked local agents on this bus can read. Write by owning agent. Our instance's shared working memory (tickets, outcomes, patterns specific to this deployment).
- **Agent DB (per device)** — Igor's memories, Granny's build history, Vetinari's task state. Owner reads/writes freely. Other agents request access via bus (inter-agent permission protocol).
- **Client DB (per human)** — Akien's, Leah's personal data. Private by default. Owner controls access grants.

A device typically connects to 3 DBs: Global (read), Local (read/write for its domain), Agent (own). Client DBs are separate per person. This replaces the current model where everything co-habits the Igor-wild-0001 Postgres instance.

Relates to T-memory-flat-table-reform — the flat-table reform should be designed against this 4-tier model from the start.

**Affected files:** TBD — discovery step. Likely: new Postgres databases or schemas per tier; connection management in skeleton or shim; access control primitives

**Design rules:** no-sqlite; Postgres; isolation by DB (not just schema or table prefix — separate DB instances for client data); inter-agent access via bus not direct DB connection

**Scope boundary:** IN — tier definitions + schema design; connection management; access control model; migration plan from current single-DB. OUT — full migration execution (separate ticket); client UI for access control; encryption at rest (follow-on)

**Completion criteria:** (1) design doc specifies 4 tiers with schema per tier; (2) connection config supports pointing each tier to a different DB; (3) agent accessing another agent's data goes via bus request, not direct query; (4) migration path from Igor-wild-0001 single DB described
